perturbation.original_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier shall be required to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The MAOV [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, contingent upon commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier may, at their discretion, purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The MAOV [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, contingent upon commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be required' to 'may, at their discretion' transforms a contractual obligation into an option. This affects the enforceability of the agreement regarding minimum purchase volumes.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-306 implies good faith in setting requirements (here, minimum order volume) and prevents opportunistic reduction of orders if the market price drops. Making the purchase volume discretionary undermines this implied obligation of good faith and proportionality.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings. (1) A term which measures the quantity by the output of the seller or the requirements of the buyer means such actual output or requirements as may occur in good faith , except that no quantity unreasonably disproportionate to any stated estimate or in the absence of a stated estimate to any normal or otherwise comparable prior output or requirements may be tendered or demanded. (2) A lawful agreement by either the seller or the buyer for exclusive dealing in the kind of goods concerned imposes unless otherwise agreed an obligation by the seller to use best efforts to supply the goods and by the buyer to use best efforts to promote their sale . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates Premier to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume (MAOV), while the modified contract makes this purchase discretionary ('may, at their discretion'). UCC § 2-306, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, implies good faith in determining requirements (like minimum order volume) and disallows opportunistic order reduction if market prices decrease. By changing 'shall be required' to 'may, at their discretion,' the altered text undermines the implied obligation of good faith and proportionality because the buyer can arbitrarily choose not to meet the MAOV, potentially contradicting the original intent and implied obligations under UCC § 2-306.
